headline: Fossil Captures Final Moments of T. rex Attack
meta_description: Scientists have discovered a fossilized Edmontosaurus skull with a Tyrannosaurus tooth embedded in its face, revealing rare evidence of a dramatic predator-pre…
summary_short: A remarkable fossil shows a Tyrannosaurus tooth still embedded in a dinosaur's skull, revealing how the giant predator hunted millions of years ago.
body:
Paleontologists have discovered a fossilized skull that captures an extraordinary moment from the prehistoric past: the skull of an Edmontosaurus with a Tyrannosaurus tooth still lodged in its face. This rare artifact provides direct physical evidence of a dramatic predator-prey encounter that unfolded millions of years ago.

The embedded tooth tells a vivid story about hunting behavior. The positioning of the tooth and the damage pattern on the skull suggest that the Tyrannosaurus delivered an incredibly powerful face-to-face bite—not a glancing blow, but a forceful attack aimed directly at the head. This discovery offers scientists new insights into how one of Earth's most feared predators actually hunted its prey.

Finds like this are exceptionally rare because they require precise conditions: the attack must happen in a way that preserves both predator and prey remains together, and those remains must survive millions of years of geological pressure and chemical changes. Each such discovery deepens our understanding of ancient ecosystems and the behaviors of creatures that dominated Earth long ago.
